title: Bridge Essay: Neurodynamical Computing at the Information Boundaries of Intelligent Systems.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind.

**Neurodynamical Computing at the Information Boundaries of Intelligent Systems.**. Cognitive computation.. PMID: 39129840. DOI: 10.1007/s12559-022-10081-9. Artificial intelligence has not achieved defining features of biological intelligence despite models boasting more parameters than neurons in the human brain. In this perspective article, we synthesize historical approaches to understanding intelligent systems and argue that methodological and epistemic biases in these fields can be resolved by shifting away from cognitivist brain-as-computer theories and recognizing that brains exist within large, interdependent living systems.
